rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALD R. BARRICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Confirmation Of Short Sale, Transfer Of Property To Buyers Free Of Liens And Conversion Of Secured Debt To Unsecured Debt
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine: 1) The petition did not explain the delay in bringing to the Court's attention the issues preventing close of escrow, after the Court confirmed sale of the subject real property more than a year ago in February 2024. 2) The petition did not cite any legal authority supporting the request to transfer title to the property free and clear of all encumbrances, notably including the 2006 deed of trust signed by the decedent to secure his obligations to repay $975,000 + interest to the creditors whose claims on file have been rejected and remain unsatisfied. See Civil Code section 2941 requiring the lenders as the deed of trust beneficiaries to execute a full reconveyance if the trustee failed to comply and also authorizing the title company to execute a release if the lenders fail to comply. 3) The petition did not cite any legal authority supporting the request to substitute petitioner as trustee under the deed of trust to execute a full reconveyance purporting to release the deed of trust. See Civil Code section 2934a requiring the majority holder of the note secured by the deed of trust to select a new trustee and execute and record a substitution of trustee. 4) The petition requests an order classing the lenders' unpaid claims with general unsecured debts, even though the estate's rejections of the lenders' claims and their failure to sue the estate on the rejected claims within the 90-day statutory period eliminated their recourse against the estate as unsecured creditors with general debts. 5) The petition did not address whether Probate Code section 10362 should apply to authorize the estate or escrow agent to deposit the remaining sale proceeds ($9,351) with the clerk and release the deed of trust as the only outstanding lien, pending a determination of how the proceeds should be allocated among the lenders, nor did the petiton address whether the estate or escrow agent should be required to file an interpleader action to release the proceeds, if they are unable or unwilling to allocate the proceeds among the lenders without court approval. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SURVIVOR'S TRUST CREATED UNDER THE JAMES FAMILY TRUST, DATED SEPTEMBER 13, 1973
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/27/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) No courtesy copies have been received by the assigned examiner, but it appears that the petition is incomplete, relying on documents attached to a previous petition, which was adjudicated. This trust is not otherwise under Court supervision; each petition must stand on its own. (4) If the principal place of administration is San Mateo, this is not the proper venue. Unless the petition is dismissed in order to file in the proper county, please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other supporting papers to the assigned examiner by 8/13/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBIN WADE SYLVESTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/14/25 for the following issues: (1) Need clarification of the statutory compensation. 2% calculation seems inaccurate. Unless the lower amount is intentional, the prayers must be modified. Despite Mr. Wade's waiver of notice, the court may require proof of service of the document to him as the distribution is less than the original prayer. Fiduciary's verified supplement clarifying the amounts of the fees and proposed distribution seems acceptable. (2) Proposed order to be resubmitted to reflect the changes in the amount in (1). Also, it needs to spell out the name and the % of distribution in the omnibus clause rather than merely referring to "the person entitled to" it. Please submit the documents seven court days prior to the hearing date.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/28/2025. The following issue must be cleared two weeks prior to the date: Under what authority can the court dismiss a petition that has already been heard and approved? If the fiduciary wishes to terminate the proceeding due to the absence of assets subject to administration following the court's approval of the surviving spousal petition, the administrator must submit a formal petition.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/18/2025. The following issues must be taken care of two weeks before the hearing date: (1) Final Inventory and Appraisal has not been filed. (2) Ex parte application (drop-off) to increase bond must be submitted. CRC 7.204. Partial 1 I&A shows the assets of the estate exceed the current bond amount. It is attorney's duty to immediately request the increase (3) The personal representative shall file either a petition for an order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code sec.12200 et seq.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF GERALD DERYAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Contest Living Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Petition is premature for the court's review as the petition failed to meet the fundamental requirements as follows: " Petition must list respondent(s). Without the information, the undersigned cannot review the sufficiency of proof of service. " Proof of service to the respondent(s). " Petition to set forth why this SF Court is the proper venue. " Petition to set forth all persons who are entitled to the notice. " A copy of the living trust(s) subject to this petition must be provided. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
